text_blocks.text: Due to geographical proximity of the
Central American countries to the U.S. which has
influences, there were several movements and
proposals by the United States during the 19th
and 20th centuries to annex some or all of the
Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras with the formerly
which had the U.S.-ruled Canal Zone territory
from 1903 to 1979, and formerly British Honduras
or Belize since 1981). However, the U.S. never acted
on these proposals from some U.S. politicians; some
of which were never delivered or considered
seriously. In 2001, El Salvador adopted the U.S.
dollar as its currency, while Panama has used it
for decades due to its ties to the Canal Zone.
